title: Two release rails fired on one tag — the downstream validator raced the upstream publisher
summary: An npm-publish workflow and a registry-listing workflow both triggered on the same v* tag. The registry validates that the npm package version exists; it ran faster than the npm publish and failed with 'package not found'.
mistake: Wiring two workflows to the same trigger when one of them validates the artifact the other produces. GitHub Actions gives no cross-workflow ordering — the lighter job wins the race, reads the world before the heavier job has changed it, and fails with a misleading 404/not-found that looks like a metadata bug.
instead: Make the dependency explicit: either poll for the upstream artifact before proceeding (loop on `npm view <pkg> version` until it equals the tag, with a hard timeout), or chain via `workflow_run` on the upstream workflow's completion, or merge both into one workflow with `needs:`. When a downstream validator reports 'not found' right after a release, suspect the race before suspecting the metadata.
